review_text:
Absolutely awesome weekend here, beach adjacent to campsite was not a swimming beach, but 3 min drive out the driveway and I was at the most beautiful beach I’ve ever seen. Facilities surprised me with hot barbecues, hot showers, flushing toilets etc.

Ps the lady at reception was super nice.

Would %100 recommend for a beach/surf trip

In a good spot to surf. Very overpriced for a patch of sandy dirt. Manager should not be working in this industry he is rude and has no customer service skills, does not know how to talk or respect the guests of the campground. Plenty of better camps for a cheaper price.

Thanks Camp Treachery! Had an amazing weekend here. Staying in the Yagon cabin. Was lovely. Being next to beach and my friends camping was great. And the staff are super kind even posting me my wallet that I left behind! Thanks
Awesome experience ! great place to go and enjoy the beauty of the nature
Beautiful campground in walking distance to a wonderful beach. Lots of wildlife around. Amenities are basic but had hot showers and were very clean. Drinking water and coin operated BBQs available
Awsome area to camp for families and within walking distance to the beach
Great spot. Beautiful grounds. Can have a fire. Great for families or couples

This campground is perfectly tucked away in the forest, offering a real nature escape. There’s plenty of wildlife around — from birds to reptiles — so it’s definitely the kind of place you’ll enjoy if you appreciate being close to nature (and don’t mind the snake sighting).

The dunes and beach nearby are absolutely amazing — wide, untouched, and perfect for a walk, a swim, or just soaking in the view.

The amenities are fairly basic and could use a little improvement, but they do the job. It would be nice to have a proper camp kitchen, though the simple setup fits the rustic charm of the place.

Overall, a peaceful and authentic bush camping experience surrounded by forest and just minutes from a stunning stretch of coastt a beautiful place!
Absolutely awesome spot, very busy due to some issues with flooding they’ve had, through no fault of their own - great spot!
Best camping spot on the east coast of Australia!! It has something for everyone, a true Aussie gem!!

Great camping spot! The timed showers have amazing pressure, it almost feels like a massage, lol. The camping grounds aren’t marked, but the areas were beautiful. We saw so much wildlife: dingoes, red-bellied snakes, goannas, and even some sea turtles at Seal Rocks.

The closest beach ( treachery ) is about a 10-minute walk and is great for surfing, but for swimming, it’s better to take a quick 5-minute drive to Seal Rocks. One of the days, we grabbed coffee from Tim’s Café, and it was pretty good too.
Had an amazing stay at Treachery campsite! Shout out to Jon for making our stay extra comfortable. Will definitely be back ☺️
Amazing place to camp. Beach, fishing, sand dunes and fires!
Jon is a legend, and all the staff are wonderful, such a beautiful paradise!
Awesome location. We campedA weekend was not long enough to take it all in. I wish I could have stayed longer! PS If you're scared of lizards, this would be excellent exposure therapy😅
Beautiful campsite right at the beach! Awesome owners
Such a great place for a beach getaway

Jon is a hero!

My wife and I recently made our way out to Oz for the first time (for our honeymoon) and TC in Seal Rock was our first stop. The location next to isolated beaches was great, as well as the vibe from the fellow campers.

The standout experience while staying there arose when our brand new rental Caravan had a faulty motor (that lowers the bed), which rendered us without a suitable sleeping location.

Jon let us use the phone as long as we needed to contact the Caravan company, and when we weren’t able to get any resolve from them, gave us a cabin with a bed during a sold out weekend.

I can’t say enough about the great Aussie hospitality over the week and a half we were in country, and Jon was at the top of the list. Hopefully we can return the favor someday!

Winter camping! Great spot with lots of nearby activities. Camp sites are unmarked and rough. Facilities are well maintained. Walk surf fish whale watch mountain bike. Great spot friendly staff and they have cabins if you want

Good place to camp if you're looking for a place near by to Seal Rocks. Not exactly close enough to walk with all your gear, but definitely a close drive to the main beaches.
$88 for 2 weekend nights for 2 people. The facilities are clean and never full, which is great.
There are no designated camping spots, it's a bit of a free for all so make sure to get there early in the day if you want a flat spot for your tent. There's a lovely sand dune to watch the sunset from and a shortish walk to a beach for swimming and surfing.
Lots of mosquitos and monitors that have no problem with boundaries.

If lizards and the occasional bird aren't an issue for you then this camp site is perfect. Very close to the beach for a good swim and nice overall location. Road to the site is a bit bumpy hence my 4 stars.

The last few ks of road once you go past the post office/shop it turns to very badly maintained dirt. Once you get to the campground the roads get better. I was camping but the cabins look really nice but I'm not sure how much they were.
It was $22 per person, all camping is unpowered. The caravan park down the road was $77 but only had powered, what a rip off.
There are only a couple of toilet shower blocks so if you don't have your own toilet you may wasn't to base your location around that.
The showers were fantastic! But are busy early evenings.
Heaps of goannas and bush turkeys. Mosquito's weren't too bad but I stayed away from the swamp in the middle just in case.
Got a little lost crossing the dune to get to the beach ha ha so many footprints going nowhere but all good I got there in the end and it was lovely, just a few surfers and a few campers sunbathing. There is vehicle access but you take a side road before you get to the park.
Ice is only $5 but the firewood was $17 for like 6 large pieces, almost more than the camping fee. (there wouldn't have been enough wood to collect)
I enjoyed my night there and would definitely go back.

Overpriced and overcrowded. Loads of people only there to party, it’s loud until very late, with people turning up after midnight to setup camp. Also considering how expensive it is, the amenities are very basic, and charging a dollar for the privilege of using the bbq is almost insulting. The camp isn’t very tidy either. There’s much better value campsites not too far away, including national parks ones.

Large camping area with a communal kitchen, flush toilets, and showers. Ice and firewood are available at the reception. You may spot some goannas and bush turkeys wandering around the campsite, but don't worry, they're harmless and just looking for a snack.

Nos alojamos durante 3 noches en un bungalow doble con capacidad para 4 personas.||La ubicación está muy bien ya que muy cerca hay unas playas increíbles. Ahora bien, los bungalows necesitan una renovación urgente en cuanto a instalaciones y mobiliario se refiere.

Camped for one night. Agree with prior reviews that the lax enforcement of site occupancy and night noise overtakes the positives (gorgeous wild beach, lots of wildlife). We were kept awake until 2am by a group of 20 drunk boomers who couldn’t have cared less. And it wasn’t just them—It’s a party campground.

Fairly disappointing, having heard lots of good things we were looking forward to a camping holiday. On arrival, the place was overbooked and poorly organised. No designated camp sites meant some people could spread out, whilst others struggled to find a level camping spot. Amenities were very basic. Very overpriced. Beautiful location and great wildlife spotting.

Would not recommend unless you are looking to surf. Much better campgrounds elsewhere. There are Alot of mosquitoes and midges. Not much air breeze. Campground was very stuffy- did camp over summer period. Not allocated sites- people tend to spread out too much at the good spots- staff do not patrol this. Alot of young surfer groups getting drunk and rowdy at night- swearing and shouting. Again staff don't monitor this.   Showers are very hot and no way of changing the temperature- though pressure is very good and ample time. Road into campsite is unkept and really should be sealed considering how many cars use it. Only beach near the campsite worth swimming if not planning to surf is lighthouse beach and sugarloaf beach. Number 1 beach was underwhelming and treachery beach not worth the walk, too rough for swimming. Also humidity in the summer period we went was 90% or higher. Wouldn't recommend camping during this period. Nights were long and hot.

Muy buen alojamiento para desconectarse y disfrutar de la naturaleza y la playa.
Fuimos con nuestra campervan y nos aparcamos en un sitio que conseguimos (no hay parcela asignada) por $50 la noche. Las instalaciones están muy bien, hay duchas con agua caliente y baños limpios.
La playa con acceso al camping es paradisiaca.
Cosas a tener en cuenta:
- No hay señal de teléfono
- No hay wifi
- El predio tiene un café
- La caminata a la playa es de 10 minutos por un sendero de dunas
- El camping tiene cabañas, carpas, y sitios para campervans
- Cuenta con BBQs y lavaderos

Hermoso camping con fácil acceso a la playa a 10 minutos por un sendero luego de subir una pequeña duna a pie.

El mejor camping en el que estuve en la costa este, por su playa inmensa alejada de los turistas; logra la combinación perfecta de sensación de naturaleza, pero con todos los ammenities necesarios, incluido un café.

Llegamos en campervan luego de las 5pm por lo que aparcamos y pagamos $50 la noche para dos personas al día siguiente.

Recomiendo totalmente. Pienso volver.

Such a beautiful, peaceful and relaxing place to visit.  Well done to all the friendly and very helpful Treachery Camp staff.  A breakfast and / or lunch of Champions can always be enjoyed at “Tim’s at Treach”.
This place is pure magic ✨
Great camping spot for people that want something a little less touristy. Cruisy spot with lots of animals. Just don't leave your food scraps as there are wild dingos that get around.

Acampamos p dormir en el auto, pagamos 44aud para dos personas, duchas de agua caliente y buenos amenities. No teniamos señal de celular o internet pero vino bien para desconectarse. Los alrededores son bellísimos, el faro y los lookuts increibles.
brrr skibidi dop dop yes yes skibidi dee dop
Too expensive for what it is. Overbooking and the bbq is not free.
Beautiful place to stay, loved it, just be aware there is no cell service.

What a beautiful hidden gem treachery camp is. Such a peaceful place with lots of wildlife. You can ether walk over the sand dunes to treachery beach or the walking track amongst the big beautiful fig trees. Short drive away from the lighthouse & other must see beaches. The staff are very friendly & helpful. Can not wait for my next visit. Great camping experience.
My favourite comping spot so far! Beautiful beach, allow fires, coffee shop, dingoes and many nice walks around.

I loved it here. We only stayed one night but that's because our Starlink kept dropping out and we had to work. It was dropping out because you'll be hard pressed to find an open spot that isn't surrounded by trees.

The park is teeming with life and the trees are beautiful. There were a couple of dingoes roaming around at night but staff were relaxed about it and didn't seem concerned, the dingoes usually keep to themselves.

A short walk to the beach and you'll see whales, lots of them (we arrived in June)

Watch out for the kookaburras pecking your window.
